observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a) Head of the GSO to prepare and submit immediately the Acceptance and Inspection Report and other supporting documents upon receipt and inspection of the items/goods purchased to the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Voucher (JV) to record the inventories in the Journal of Procurements Received and in the inventory, ledger cards;
b) Head of the GSO to prepare and submit the monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) together with the supporting Issue Slip to the Municipal Accountant within five days after the end of the month as the basis in drawing a JV to record the issuances; and
c) Municipal Accountant to record in the appropriate Inventory account all non-consumable purchases of supplies and materials upon acceptance and inspection of the delivered items and only recognize the expense upon consumption or distribution for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: The GSO shall submit the acceptance and inspection report and other supporting documents upon receipt and inspection of the items/goods purchased to the Municipal Accountant's Office. The GSO and the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the supporting documents to the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary Journal Voucher to record the inventories in the Journal of Procurements Received and in the inventory ledger cards. Otherwise, the transactions shall be suspended in audit.
observations.reason: The necessity of immediate distribution of goods related to COVID-19 and the skeletial work arrangements during the ECQ were the reason for failure to comply.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWMC to coordinate with the NSWMC for the guidelines in conduction of the revised Waste Analysis and Characterization Study (WACS) immediately to address the increasing volume of wastes within the locality for environmental sanitation and public health safety.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the MSWMC to coordinate with the NSWMC for the guidelines in conduction the revised Waste Analysis and Characterization Study (WACS).
observations.reason: Still waiting for the new guidelines in the conduct of updating the WACS from the NSWMC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and other officials concerned to undertake an immediate, effective, efficient and aggressive tax collection efforts and develop strategies to improve their tax collection efficiency. Avail legal remedies such as administrative and judicial actions to enforce collections from delinquent real property taxpayers, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Tax campaigns are being undertaken by the Municipality in partnership with the Provincial Treasurer’s Office.
observations.reason: The delinquent real property taxes ballooned in the amount of R242,048,916.46 on February 9, 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant to demand from the Municipal Treasurer for the
immediate submission of the related disbursement vouchers and other
supporting documents, and subsequently draw journal entries to record all valid
reconciling items for a fair presentation of the balance of Cash in Bank-LCCA
account in the financial statements;
b. Municipal Treasurer to monitor the submission of all paid disbursement
vouchers and other supporting documents to the Municipal Accounting Office
for recording to present the true/correct balances of the accounts in the financial
statements; and
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, strictly comply with the
provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2,1996.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal General Services Officer (MSGO) to prepare and submit immediately
the Acceptance and Inspection Report and other supporting documents upon
receipt and inspection of the items/goods purchased to the Municipal
Accountant to draw a Journal Voucher (JV) to record the inventories in the
Journal of Procurements Received and in the inventory ledger cards;
b. MGSO to prepare and submit the monthly Report of Supplies and Materials
Issued (RSMI) together with the supporting Requisition Issue Slip to the
Municipal Accountant within five days after the end of the month as basis in
drawing a JV to record the issuances;
c. Municipal Accountant to record in the appropriate Inventory account all
purchases of supplies and materials upon acceptance and inspection of the
delivered items and only recognize the expense upon consumption or
distribution for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statement; and
d. concerned municipal official to submit the lacking supporting documents and
a written explanation on the deficiencies noted to substantiate the
disbursement made and facilitate the post audit of the same, Otherwise, the said
transactions shall be suspended in audit.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct
the:
a. Inventory Committee to complete the inventory count to prove the existence of
the Municipality’s properties, prepare and submit to the Auditor the RPCPPE
in the prescribed form. Prioritize the reconciliation of the results of the inventory
count with the property and accounting records. Any discrepancies found should be
investigated, and the necessary adjustment should be made where the error was
committed;
b. GSO to update the Property Cards for all PPE accounts and the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards for each category of
assets and Real Property Ledger Cards for land to substantiate the correctness
of the general ledger balances for PPE accounts; and
c. concerned Municipal Officials to submit a request for relief from property
accountability and all the required documents for the flooded PPE to the
Commission on Audit to facilitate approval thereof so that the cost of the said
items be dropped from the books of account.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant to:
a. request for an authority for the reversion of those payables without valid
documentation in the amount of P548,625.72 and have been outstanding for
more than two years, to the Sangguniang Bayan. Thereafter, make the necessary
journal entry to revert the said long outstanding obligations to Government
Equity so that the Municipality can use the available funds for other priority
projects that would uplift the living condition of its constituents;
b. submit to the Municipal Treasurer those obligations found to be valid and
properly supported by sufficient evidence for immediate payment; and
c. record only payables with proper and complete documentation.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate
with OlC-Provincial Accountant and Provincial Treasurer to verify the net
differences of P(623,264.33) and P(1,665,030.52) between the recorded balances
of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their reciprocal
accounts Due to and Due from LGUs reflected in the books of the Province;
b. instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to subsequently prepare the required
adjusting journal entries to present the correct balances of the said accounts in
the Municipality’s financial statements; and
c. require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to conduct a periodic reconciliation of
accounts Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs in the Municipality books with the
corresponding reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the liquidation reports of the
fund transfers to DOH-Region IVA and DA-CO;
b. Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the OIC- Municipal Accountant to remit
to the BTr/source agencies all the unused balance of funds pursuant to the cited
regulations, so that the amount could be immediately used for other projects/
programs of the National Government and to relieve the Municipality from any
accountability/liability for said unused funds; and
c. henceforth, observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 in implementing
projects funded by Source Agencies to ensure that funds are utilized and
liquidated on time and the delivery of services and benefits to the public will be
facilitated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to request
the system provider to furnish the Municipality with a duly certified list of taxpayers
indicating the amount due or collectible from each. The document to be provided at
the start of each year will serve as the basis for setting up the Real Property Tax/
Special Education Tax Receivables in compliance with Section 20 of the MNGAS for
LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Local Finance Committee to prepare the Monthly Report of Fund
Utilization and Status of P/P/A in accordance with the required format until the
said fund are fully liquidated and submit the same to the Audit Team and to other
offices pursuant to Item 4.4 of LBC No. 136; and
b. instruct the concerned official to post the revised reports on the Municipality’s
website in order to properly disclose the related information regarding the
utilization and disbursements of the aforesaid financial assistance in public.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to:
a. ensure the optimum utilization of the 20% DF thru efficient implementation of all
priority development projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to
help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development of
the Municipality; and
b. properly plan the timely procurement and implementation of all identified
priority development projects under the 20% DF to guarantee the prompt
accomplishment of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. GFPS to maximize the utilization/implementation of the GPB to attain its
objective in accordance with Item 2.3 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-
01;
b. GFPS Executive Committee and TWG to monitor the effective and efficient
implementation of the GAD PPAs and the judicious utilization of the GAD funds;
and
c. GFPS to prepare GAD PPAs budget judiciously so as not to exceed the project’s
appropriation during implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer and the OIC- Municipal Accountant to submit to the
Audit Team: (a) Report on the Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF sourced
from NDRRMF; and (b) Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations
and correspondingly publish the same on the website of the Municipality as
required under Items V.A.1 and V.C of COA Circular No. 2014-002;
b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to include information pertaining to DRRM
procurement, issuance/transfer of Inventories and PPE out of NDRRMF and
cash donations to the Notes to Financial Statements as required under Item VII.2
of COA Circular No. 2014-002; and
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the
affected accounts, by the following entry:
Debit Credit
Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment 449,875.00
Government Equity 449,875.00
For the account Welfare Goods for Distribution, Medical Dental/Laboratory
Supplies Inventory and Other Supplies and Material Inventory the amount to be
recorded will be the remaining balance of the goods in stock, if there’s any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a) Municipal Accounting Office to obtain from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office all the necessary documents needed to support the identified reconciling items and draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment in the General Ledger for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements;
b) Municipal Accounting and Treasurer’s Offices to conduct the necessary periodic reconciliation of their respective records to ensure accuracy in recording. Make the necessary adjustments, if there are any, to avoid accumulation of the discrepancies between the two records and to present the correct balance of the Cash in Bank-Local Currency Current Account in the financial statements; and
c) Municipal Treasurer’s Office to submit all paid disbursement vouchers to the Municipal Accounting Office for recording so that the true/correct balances of the accounts for the period are properly presented in the financial statements and to avoid the loss or misplacement of the vouchers. Make proper demand from the concerned taxpayers for the settlement of the returned/dishonored checks.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant obtained the necessary documents needed from the Municipal Treasurer to record valid unrecorded reconciling items (paid disbursement vouchers) to support the identified reconciling items and draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment in the General Ledger. The Municipal Accountant prepared the necessary reconciliation between the two records. The Municipal Treasurer continues to retrieve and reconstruct the missing documents.
observations.reason: Some prior year’s reconciling items still occur as at year-end, like stale checks and unrecorded disbursement vouchers.

observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted the Physical Inventory Report and submitted the Inventory Report. Coordination with the Municipal Engineering Office for status of each project reported under Construction in Progress has been undertaken by the GSO Inventory Team regarding the Road Network. The Municipal Accountant provided the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher Nos. 300-2015-332, 300-2015-333 & 300-2105-334 for the completed projects under Construction in Progress. Municipal Engineering Office submitted report last January 29, 2021 which was already authenticated both the Municipal Accountant and GSO Property. Notes to FS Road Network System was done.
observations.reason: The inventory undertaken was incomplete.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a) request for an authority for the reversion of those payables without valid documentation in the amount of P1,095,810.30 and have been outstanding for more than two years, to the Sangguniang Bayan. Thereafter, make the necessary journal entry to revert the said long outstanding obligations to Government Equity so that the Municipality can use the available funds for other priority projects that would uplift the living condition of its constituents;
b) submit to the Municipal Treasurer those obligations found to be valid and properly supported by sufficient evidence for immediate payment; and
c) record only payables with proper and complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor requested to the Sangguniang Bayan for an authority for reversion of payables without valid documentation. The Municipal Accountant submitted to the Treasurer’s Office some of the obligations with valid supporting documents for payment. The Municipality initiated actions to address the payables cited.
observations.reason: Outstanding Accounts Payable for more than two years amounting to P548,625.72 was still in the book.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to regularly coordinate with the OlC-Provincial Accountant to reconcile the recorded balances of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their reciprocal accounts Due to and Due from LGUs reflected in the books of the Province; and subsequently prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for the noted difference, to present the true balance of the said accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate the old files/records of Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts. Make a representation with the concerned agencies to formally inquire whether there were unremitted balances in their records for 2014 and prior years. In case the Municipality has no unremitted balance for the said period, request for a certification of no-arrears; and subsequently request for approval from Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of the said Inter-Agency Payables accounts to the Unappropriated Surplus. The Municipal Accountant prepared and submitted the reconciliation of accounts to the OlC-Provincial Accountant thru messenger dated February 10, 2022 but no reply was received yet.
observations.reason: For CY 2014 reconciling items.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
direct the:

a. Municipal Accounting Office to obtain from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office all
the necessary documents needed (paid disbursement vouchers) to support the
identified reconciling items and draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record all
valid reconciling items that require adjustment in the General Ledger for fair
presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements;

b. Municipal Accounting and Treasurer’s Offices to conduct periodic
reconciliation of their records to ensure accuracy in recording. Make the
necessary adjustments, if there are any, to avoid accumulation of the
discrepancies between the two records and to present the correct balance of
the Cash in Bank-Local Currency Current Account in the financial statements; and

c. Municipal Treasurer’s Office to submit all paid disbursement vouchers to the
Municipal Accounting Office for recording so that the true/correct balances of
the accounts for the period are properly presented in the financial statements
and to avoid the loss or misplacement of the vouchers. Make proper demand
from the concerned taxpayers for the settlement of the returned/dishonored
checks.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
direct the:

a. Inventory Committee to complete the inventory count to prove the existence of
the Municipality’s properties, prepare and submit to the Auditor the RPCPPE in
the prescribed form. Prioritize the reconciliation of the results of the inventory
count with the property and accounting records. Any discrepancies found
should be investigated, and the necessary adjustment should be made where
the error was committed;

b. Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare the necessary journal entry for
the reclassification of the completed project to the appropriated PPE account
and henceforth, coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office for status of
each projects reported under Construction in Progress- Land Improvements
account.

c. Concerned Municipal Officials to submit a request for relief from property
accountability and all the required documents for the flooded Work/Zoo Animal
to the Commission on Audit to facilitate approval thereof so that the cost be
dropped from the books of accounts; and

d. Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Engineering Office on the
unreconciled amount on the submitted data on the road network system
account so that proper adjustment and disclosure in the Notes to Financial
Statements on the total road network system of the Municipality at year-end
can be made.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant to:

a. request for an authority for the reversion of those payables without valid
documentation in the amount of P1,095,810.30 and have been outstanding for
more than two years, to the Sangguniang Bayan. Thereafter, make the
necessary journal entry to revert the said long outstanding obligations to
Government Equity so that the Municipality can use the available funds for
other priority projects that would uplift the living condition of its constituents;

b. submit to the Municipal Treasurer those obligations found to be valid and
properly supported by sufficient evidence for immediate payment; and

c. record only payables with proper and complete documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
regularly coordinate with the OlC-Provincial Accountant to reconcile the recorded
balances of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their
reciprocal accounts Due to and Due from LGUs reflected in the books of the
Province; and subsequently prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for
the noted difference, to present the true balance of the said accounts in the
financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Head of the GSO to prepare and submit immediately the Acceptance and
Inspection Report and other supporting documents upon receipt and
inspection of the items/goods purchased to the Municipal Accountant to draw
a Journal Voucher (JV) to record the inventories in the Journal of
Procurements Received and in the inventory ledger cards;

b. Head of the GSO to prepare and submit the monthly Report of Supplies and
Materials Issued (RSMI) together with the supporting Report Issue Slip to the
Municipal Accountant within five days after the end of the month as the basis
in drawing a JV to record the issuances; and

c. Municipal Accountant to record in the appropriate Inventory account all
purchases of supplies and materials upon acceptance and inspection of the
delivered items and only recognize the expense upon consumption or
distribution for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statement.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require
the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal
Accountant, at start of the year a certified list of the names of taxpayers and the
corresponding amount of taxes due from them at the start of the year in order that
an accurate and reliable RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income can
be taken up in the books of accounts for a fair presentation of the actual financial
position of the Municipality and to provide Management with accurate information
as a basis for decision making.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
exert extra effort to locate the old files/records of Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and
PhilHealth accounts. Make a representation with the concerned agencies to
formally inquire whether there were unremitted balances in their records for 2014
and prior years. In case the Municipality has no unremitted balance for the said
period, request for a certification of no-arrears; and subsequently request for
approval from Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of the said Inter-Agency
Payables accounts to the Unappropriated Surplus.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) Municipal Accounting Office to obtain from the Treasurer’s Office all the necessary documents needed (paid disbursement vouchers; Debit/Credit Memo) and draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record all valid reconciling items that need to adjust in the General Ledger for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements; b) Municipal Accounting and Treasurer’s Offices to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records to ensure accuracy in recording. Make the necessary adjustments, if there are any, to avoid accumulation of the discrepancies between the two records and to present the correct balance of the CIB - LCCA in the financial statements; and c) Municipal Treasurer’s Office to submit all paid disbursement vouchers to the Municipal Accounting Office for recording so that the true/correct balances of the accounts for the period are properly presented in the financial statements and to avoid the loss or misplacement of the vouchers. Make proper demand from the concerned taxpayers for the settlement of the returned/dishonored checks.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that the Municipal Accounting and Treasurer’s Offices were doing the required reconciliation on a monthly basis since time immemorial.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the partial implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) Inventory Committee to conduct a physical inventory of all the Municipality’s PPE and prepare and submit the required report thereon. Any discrepancies between the inventory report and the accounting and property records should be investigated and the necessary adjustment should be made where the error was committed; b) GSO to determine the officials/ employees to whom the equipment was issued, and issue the corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for each property to pinpoint the direct person accountable and responsible thereon; c) Municipal Accountant to provide the correct depreciation and make the necessary adjustment for each type of properties of the Municipality; d) Municipal Engineer to expedite the submission of the complete description and cost segregation of road components for the road projects of the Municipality; e) GSO to maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and LRNPC and RLRN in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008; and f) Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the total road network system as required in COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor, thru the Property Officer and Municipal Accountant, commented that the Inventory Committee had been using the RPCPPE format from the previous New Government Accounting Standards (NGAS) for LGUs and that they are now in the transition of using the new prescribed form of RPCPPE as per COA Circular No. 2020-004, Volume II. Further, the amount of P424,769.57 for the Construction in Progress has been reclassified as Water Supply System as evidenced by Journal Entry Voucher No. 300-2105-332, 333 and 334. Moreover, Report on Lost, Stolen, Damaged and Destroyed Property (RLSDDP) for Work/Zoo Animals had been accomplished and the Municipality has crafted Request for Relief of Accountability Form (RRAF) to be use in facilitating request from relief of accountability. Lastly, they commented that the unreconciled amount on the submitted Road Network Report of Engineering Office (Furnished to the Municipal Accountant and Commission on Audit last January 29, 2021) is in the process of validation and authentication by both the Municipal Accountant and GSO Property Unit and adjustment in the Notes to Financial Statements will be done as soon as the process of validation and reconciliation of the report has been concluded.
observations.reason: The inventory undertaken was incomplete. Reiterated in audit observation no. 2 page no. 30

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a) request for an authority for the reversion of those payables without valid documentation in the amount of P696,977.58 and have been outstanding for two years or more, to the Sangguniang Bayan. Thereafter, make the necessary journal entry to revert the said long outstanding obligations to Government Equity so that the Municipality can use the available funds for other priority projects that would uplift the living condition of its constituents; b) submit to the Municipal Treasurer those obligations found to be valid and properly supported by sufficient evidence for immediate payment; and c) record only payables with proper and complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor, thru the Municipal Accountant, stated that adjustments were already made to revert the Accounts Payable from CYs 2004 to 2016 in the amount of P696,977.58 per Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 027, s. 2021 dated January 29, 2021.
observations.reason: Request for the reversion of Accounts Payable of £696,977.58 was approved but it was reverted only in February 2021. For CY 2020 FS, the said AP was still in the book.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the OIC-Provincial Accountant to reconcile the recorded balances of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their reciprocal accounts Due to and Due from LGUs reflected in the books of the Province; and subsequently prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for the noted difference, to present the true balance of the said accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor, thru the Municipal Accountant, informed the Audit Team that upon reconciling their books of accounts with the Province’s books, they found the reconciling item not yet recorded as income in the end of year 2020. Also, they committed to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Reconciling items adjusted for 2019 was adjusted. But for 2020 accounts, still there are reconciling items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting journal entry to correct the error committed in recording the funds transfer from source agencies amounting to P1,057,910.28; b) Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to remit to the BTr/source agencies the unused balance of funds in various agencies so that the amount could be immediately used for other programs/projects of the National Government and to relieve the Municipality from any accountability/liability; and c) Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledger of funds transfer from source agencies per source Projects/Programs/Activities (PPAs) for easily monitoring of funds transferred to the Municipality and to avoid error in recording of transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor, thru the Municipal Accountant, informed the Audit Team that upon reconciling their books of accounts with the Province’s books, they found the reconciling item not yet recorded as income in the end of year 2020. Also, they committed to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: For some items adjustments were made and some amounts were remitted. But some supporting documents and full compliance not yet provided. For 2019 transactions some reconciling items adjusted; For 2020 accounts still reconciling items exist.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned municipal officials to strictly adhere with the above cited provision of COA Circular No. 2009- 01 on the submission of contracts/POs, together with pertinent documents for timely and objective evaluation of the same by the Audit Team, so that the Municipality could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the BAC Secretariat and Procurement Team to submit all perfected contracts/Purchase Orders and its supporting documents forming part thereof in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the lacking supporting documents to attest the propriety and validity of the subject transactions; b) provide explanation on the deficiencies noted; and c) ensure that all disbursements of the Municipality are duly supported with documentation before processing the same for payment to avoid audit suspensions and/or audit disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Disbursements were supported with documentation. However, there was still some transaction with incomplete documentation. Explanation on deficiencies provided.
observations.reason: Some transactions still had incomplete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) Municipal officials concerned to strictly adhere to the guidelines provided in the Joint Memorandum Circulars to invest in high-impact or development projects that would improve the socio-economic condition of the constituents; and b) Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate fund in the General Fund Proper to replenish the amount of 20% DF utilized for projects which do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures and not included among the allowable projects under the 20% DF. Otherwise, the payments shall be disallowed in audit. Further, refrain from using the 20% DF for purposes other than those provided in DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-01.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Budget Officer to strictly adhere to Joint Memorandum Circular in the utilization of the 20% Development Plan.
observations.reason: No management action provided for some items; expenditures which do not partake the nature of investment/capital were still included. Reiterated in audit observation no. 10 page no. 49

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GFPS to: a) fully implement the planned GAD program, projects and activities to ensure that the GAD objectives are fully attained; b) strictly monitor the implementation of the planned GAD program, projects and activities to ensure that the GAD objectives are fully achieved; and c) submit justification on the overspending of P9,533,498.50.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the GAD Focal Person and all concerned departments and offices to fully implement GAD Plan and Budget and monitor its implementation. The Municipal Budget Officer submitted justification last July 8, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) MDRRMO to prepare a comprehensive LDRRMFIP including the list of projects and activities to be funded out of the previous year’s unexpended LDRRMF to ensure Municipality’s capacity to respond and recover from the impacts of disasters and emergencies that may occur; and b) Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly report on the source of utilization of the LDRRMF through the LDRRMC and LDC to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly report on the source of utilization of the LDRRMF through the LDRRMC and LDC to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) MDRRMO to ensure the efficient and effective implementation of all response programs and projects identified during the year to optimize the utilization of fund and guarantee that the objectives of reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities will be achieved; b) MDRRMO to refrain from charging expenses not in line with the established purpose of QRF; and c) Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursements of the Municipality are duly supported with proper documentation before processing the same for payment, to avoid audit suspensions and/or audit disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO submitted justification to the Audit Team in response to AOM No. 2020-12(19) dated September 3, 2020. The MDRRMO instructed the MDRRMO to ensure that all PPA’s under LDRRMF for CY 2019 were fully implemented though there are supervising events that do not require the disbursement of fund. For 2019 transactions all PPA’s under LDRRMF for CY 2019 were fully implemented though there are events that do not require disbursement from LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Some documents still awaited for submission; some disbursements had incomplete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWMC to coordinate with the NSWMC for the guidelines in conduction the updating of Waste Analysis and Characterization Study (WACS).
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the MSWMC through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to conduct the updating of Waste Analysis and Characterization Study (WACS).
observations.reason: The MSWMP undertaken was incomplete. Reiterated in audit observation no. 14 page no. 53

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant, at the start of the year a certified list of the names of the taxpayers and the corresponding amount of taxes due and collectible to establish the correct and valid RPT and SET.
observations.managementAction: The service provider, Amellar Solutions had been instructed to customized the system that will generate the correct and valid RPT and SET.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MSWB to create a Technical Working Group to assist the Board to expedite the revision/update of the MSWMP in order to effectively and efficiently address the above-cited solid waste management problems.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the MSWB through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Coordinator to conduct the updating of Waste Analysis and Characterization Study (WACS).
observations.reason: Still waiting for the new guidelines in the conduct of WACS from the NSWMC.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a) Municipal Accounting Office to obtain from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office all the necessary documents needed (paid disbursement vouchers; Debit/Credit Memo) to support the identified reconciling items and draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment in the General Ledger for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements;
b) Municipal Accounting and Treasurer’s Offices to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records to ensure accuracy in recording. Make the necessary adjustments. if there are any. to avoid accumulation of the discrepancies between the two records and to present the correct balance of the CIB - LCCA in the financial statements; and
c) Municipal Treasurer’s Office to submit all paid disbursement vouchers to the Municipal Accounting Office for recording so that the true/correct balance of the accounts for the period were properly presented in the financial statements and to avoid the loss or misplacement of the vouchers. Make proper demand from the concerned taxpayers for the settlement of the returned/dishonored checks.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a) request for an authority for the reversion of those payables without valid documentation in the amount of P696,977.58 and have been outstanding for two years or more, to the Sangguniang Bayan. Thereafter, make the necessary journal entry to revert the said long outstanding obligations to Government Equity so that the Municipality can use the available funds for other priority projects that would uplift the living condition of its constituents;
b) submit to the Municipal Treasurer those obligations found to be valid and properly supported by sufficient evidence for immediate payment; and
c) record only payables with proper and complete documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a certified list of the names of the taxpayers and the corresponding amount of taxes due and collectible to establish the correct and valid RPT and SET Receivables that were recognized in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate the old files/records of Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts. Make a representation with the concerned agencies to formally inquire whether there were unremitted balances in their records for 2014 and prior years. In case the Municipality has no unremitted balance for the said period, request for a certification of no-arrears; and subsequently request for approval for the reversion of the said Inter-Agency Payables accounts to the Sangguniang Bayan.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts so that appropriate adjustments can be made on the affected accounts for fair presentation in the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a) Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting journal entry to correct the error committed in recording the funds transfer from source agencies amounting to P1,057,910.28;
b) Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to remit to the BTr/source agencies the unused balance of funds in the amount of P367,931.45 so that the amount could be immediately used for other programs/projects of the National Government and to relieve the Municipality from any accountability/liability for the said unused funds; and
c) Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledger of funds transfer from source agencies per Projects/Programs/Activities for easily monitoring of funds transferred to the Municipality and to avoid error in recording of transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned municipal officials to strictly adhere with the above cited provision of COA Circular No. 2009-01 on the submission of contracts/POs. together with pertinent documents for timely and objective evaluation of the same by the Audit Team. so that the Municipality could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the lacking supporting documents to attest the propriety and validity of the subject transactions;
b) provide explanation on the deficiencies noted; and
c) ensure that all disbursements of the Municipality are duly supported with required documentation before processing the same for payment to avoid audit suspensions and/or audit disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a) Municipal officials concerned to strictly adhere to the guidelines provided under the Joint Memorandum Circulars to invest in high-impact or development projects that would improve the socio-economic condition of the constituents; and
b) Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate fund in the General Fund Proper to replenish the amount of 20% DF utilized for projects which do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures and not among the allowable projects under the 20% DF. Otherwise. the payments shall be disallowed in audit. Further. refrain from using the 20% DF for purposes other than those provided in DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-01.

observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Committee conducted the Physical Inventory of Property, Plant and Equipment and submitted the Inventory Report. Reiterated in audit observation no. 2 page no. 29
GSO determined to whom the PPEs were issued and currently updating issuance of AREs. Implemented
Coordination with the Municipal Engineering Office has been under-taken by the GSO Inventory Team regarding the Road Network
observations.reason:
The inventory undertaken was incomplete.
Updating of AREs are still on going.
The Municipal Engineer did not provide list of road network with complete description and cost segregation to be used as basis by the GSO in inventory taking.
No action has been made by Management to disclose the total road network system in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a) Municipal Treasurer to immediately transmit the missing unrecorded disbursement vouchers with its supporting documents to the Municipal Accounting Office and regularly monitor the outstanding checks and inform the Municipal Accountant of all stale checks for appropriate adjustment in the accounting record. In case of failure to submit the unrecorded disbursement vouchers, cause the suspension of payment of her salaries until she shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission pursuant to and Section 122(2) of PD No. 1445;
b) Municipal officials concerned to explain/justify their failure to file within the reglementary period, a request for relief from accountability to the Auditor concerned for those documents/ reports that were damaged by typhoon pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 92-751 dated February 24, 1992; and
c) Municipal Accountant to adjust the identified reconciling items in the books of the Municipality to come up with the correct account balance of the Cash in Bank account in the books.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant stated that the unrecorded disbursement vouchers of P1,655,649.25 were transmitted by the Municipal Treasurer to the Accounting Office only in January 2020. While disbursement vouchers amounting to P42,069.75 are transactions from CYs 1995, 2009 to 2011 that are not yet submitted to the Office of the Municipal Accountant. And the Debit Memo amounting to P9,000.00 for the cost of check booklets were already recorded.
Further, the Municipal Treasurer commented that she was not informed by the previous Municipal Treasurer about the dishonored checks from CYs 2002 to 2007 amounting to P15,731.50. The Stale Checks for CY 2019 are gradually transmitted to the Office of the Municipal Accountant and recorded as Accounts Payable.
observations.reason: Some documents were damaged by Typhoon Ondoy and the Municipal Treasurer is having a hard time to retrieve or reconstruct the documents, while other documents were not properly turned over by the former Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a) Continue to exert extra effort to trace back prior years’ records and documents to substantiate the validity of Accounts Payable amounting to P2,887,754.35;
b) Submit to the Municipal Treasurer those obligations found to be valid and properly supported by sufficient evidence for immediate payment; and
c) Request for an authority for the reversion of those payables without valid documentation that have been outstanding for two years or more to the Sangguniang Bayan. Thereafter, make the necessary journal entry to revert the said long outstanding obligations to Government Equity so that the Municipality can use the available funds for other priority projects that would uplift the living condition of its constituents; d) Record only payables with proper and complete documentation; and e) Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to reverse the accounting entry made to recognize as liability the portion of the cost of the projects that was not yet accomplished at year-end.
observations.managementAction:
100% of the Accounts Payable has already been reverted in the book of accounts.
The Municipal Accountant submitted to the Treasurer’s Office some of the obligations with valid and proper supporting documents for immediate payment.
Sangguniang Bayan already approved the reversion of payables without valid documentation as per SB Resolution No. 101 s. 2019. The Municipal Accountant prepared the adjusting entry to reverse the entry recognizing as liability the portion of the cost of the projects that was not yet accomplished at year-end.
observations.reason: There are still claims maintained in the books with more than two years and more.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to verify the differences of P1,010,796.53 and P4,117,631.32 between the recorded balance of Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their reciprocal accounts (Due to and Due from LGUs) reflected in the Province’s books; and subsequently prepare the necessary journal entries to present the true balance of the said accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The record with the Provincial Accountant and Municipal Accountant had been reconciled as of July 31, 2019 for the year-end balance of 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC to:
a) properly plan and prioritize the projects for study and inclusion in the 20% Development Fund;
b) ensure that development projects for inclusion in the 20% DF have gone through a thorough study and priority ranking; and
c) continue to invest in high-impact or developmental projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction:
The remaining projects were being implemented.
The Management is trying their best to prioritize the development projects that be included in the DF.
The Management is trying to include the priority projects and submitted to the DILG for funding under LGSF-AM.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) direct the Municipal Engineer and the LGSF-AM Focal Person to strictly monitor the implementation of the above-cited projects to immediately address the issue that may arise during its implementation and to avoid additional delays in the completion of the same; and
b) require the Municipal Engineer and the MPDC to properly identify and prioritize the projects to be submitted to DILG for funding under LGSF-AM in the future to ensure the timely implementation of the said projects for the benefits of the general public.
observations.managementAction:
The seven remaining projects were being implemented.
The Municipal Engineer and the MPDC identified the priority projects and submitted to the DILG for funding under LGSF-AM.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to:
a) ensure that all programs, plans and activities are properly planned and that their implementation thereof be in accordance with the approved GAD Plan and Budget to optimize the use of GAD allocated funds, and that all issues on gender mainstreaming are duly addressed by the prepared plan, for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries; and
b) furnish the Audit Team a copy of the GAD AR within five working days from the end of January of preceding year, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-001.
observations.managementAction: All GAD PPA’s were already implemented, however, there were excess budget out of the budgeted amount. The Municipal Mayor instructed the GAD Focal Person and all concerned departments and employees to comply with the audit recommendation. The GAD PPA’s were built-in within the Annual Budget levels of the municipality, thus the excess expenditures refers to the regular programs, projects and activities of the LGU.
observations.reason: GAD PPAs were implemented partially.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a) LDRRMO to include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in the LDRRMFIP; and
b) Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF through the LDRRMC and LDC to the Audit Team in accordance with the period prescribed by the above-cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: Separate LDRRMFIP for CYs 2017 and 2018 were prepared and submitted to the Audit Team. Timely submission has been done regularly to DILG and Audit Team.
observations.reason: Unexpended LDRRMFIP for CY 2017 were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the above-mentioned unremitted taxes and henceforth remit all taxes withheld within the prescribed period to the Bureau of Internal Revenue. Otherwise, any surcharges, interests and penalties that may be incurred shall be the personal liability of the Municipal officials concerned who are responsible for the delay or non-remittance of the taxes.
observations.managementAction: The unremitted Taxes withheld for CY 2018 were already remitted on 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Local Chief
Executive direct the:

a) Inventory Committee to conduct a complete inventory of PPE and
prepare and submit a report thereon to the Auditor;

b) GSO and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the inventory report
with their respective records, determine the cause of any variance and
make the necessary adjustments where the error was committed;

¢) GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain a complete and updated
Property Cards and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards,
respectively, and issue ARE to pinpoint accountability based on the
reconciled inventory report of PPE; and

d) Disposal Committee to take immediate action on the disposal of the
unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration of the same and to
generate additional income from its disposal.

We reiterated our prior year's recommendations that the Local Chief
Executive direct the:

a) Municipal Treasurer to immediately transmit the unrecorded
disbursement vouchers with its supporting documents to the Municipal
Accountant’s Office; and

b) Municipal Accountant to adjust the identified reconciling items in the
books of the Municipal Government to come up with the correct account
balance of the Cash in Bank account in the books.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to use the duly certified list of
taxpayers showing the names of the taxpayers and amount due and collectible
from each of them as provided by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer at the
start of each year to serve as basis in setting-up the Real Property Tax/Special
Education Tax Receivables, in compliance with Section 20 of the Manual on
NGAS for LGUs, Volume L.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive file the request for
authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts to the COA Audit Team
Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA). The request shall be supported
by the following documents:

a. Schedule of dormant accounts by accountable officer/debtor/government
entity and by account, certified by the Accountant and approved by the
Head of the government entity;

b. Certification by the responsible officials of the entity to the effect that
there are no records/documents available to validate claim;

c. Other justifications, like in the case of request for write-off due to loss of
documents, the circumstances of the loss should be stated in the letter-
request;

d. Secure authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to write-off the receivable
accounts; and

e. Authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to write-off.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Local Chief
Executive direct the:

a) Municipal Accountant to reconcile the recorded loan balance with those
recorded in the books of the creditor agency; and

b) OIC-Municipal Treasurer to regularly coordinate with the DOF-MDFO to
determine the cause/s of the difference between the Municipality and
. DOF-MDFO records. Thereafter, inform the Municipal Accountant if
there are necessary adjustment/s to correct the errors committed in the
books of the Municipal Government in order to reflect the correct amount
of the outstanding obligation of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

- 1. BuB/ADM Focal Person to strictly monitor the implementation of the
projects under the LGSF-ADM Program; and

= 2. Municipal Engineer to properly plan the construction period required for
the implementation of the projects in order to avoid delays in the future,

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team all the lacking supporting
documents mentioned above relative to the procurement of land charged
against the SEF to substantiate the disbursements made. Otherwise, the said
transactions shall be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

2. Municipal GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare
the road map of the Municipality to facilitate the preparation of a
proper inventory and reporting of the Municipality’s road network
system;

b. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects; and thereafter; and

¢. Municipal Accountant to record the amount of road networks equivalent
to the required transition provision percentage every year starting 2016
up to 2019.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to
submit the approved Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPPB in printed
and electronic format within the prescribed period in accordance with the
provision of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive direct the:

a) Bids and Awards Committee to send out written invitations to COA and
at least two other observers at least three calendar days before the date of the
procurement stage/activity to be witnessed; and

b) BAC Secretariat to be compliant in posting procurement/bidding notices
in the PhilGEPS and in the Municipality’s website as required by
RA No. 9184 to attain the widest circulation of the agency’s
procurement/bidding activities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a) Municipal Treasurer to remit in full the withheld GSIS contributions/
premiums/loan amortizations from employees compensations within

the prescribed period; and

b) Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Due to GSIS account records
with the GSIS to determine any errors in recording thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned
Municipal Officials to consider the application of payroll payment system
through the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) facility of government banks.
The ATM payroll system provides convenience on the part of the Municipality
since payroll payments are distributed from the payroll account maintained in
an authorized government depository bank to the individual account of the
employees and job order personnel. The system will eliminate risk of loss or
misapplication of funds.

Reiterated
Finding No. 5

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Instructs the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete inventory of PPE and
prepare and submit a report thereon to the Auditor;

b) Directs the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the inventory report
with their respective records, determine the cause of any variance and make the
necessary adjustments where the error was committed; and

¢) Instructs the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain a complete and
updated Property Cards and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards,
respectively, and issue ARE to pinpoint accountability based on the reconciled
inventory report of PPE.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive instructs the GSO to immediately
facilitate the preparation and submission of the list of insurable PPEs so that the
necessary insurance coverage for all the assets can be applied with the GSIS
under the General Insurance Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct a thorough review
and analysis of the Property, Plant and Equipment account particularly those
pertaining to 2002 and prior years’ acquisitions. Continue to trace back prior years’
transactions in order to establish the details of the accounts so that the depreciation
for the same could be correctly computed and make the necessary corrections/adjustments
in the books of accounts for the inaccurate computation of depreciation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Directs the Municipal Treasurer to immediately transmit the unrecorded
disbursement vouchers with its supporting documents to the Municipal
Accountant’s Office; and

< b) Instructs the Municipal Accountant to adjust the identified reconciling items in
the books of the Municipal Government to come up with the correct account
balance of the Cash in Bank account in the books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

1. Instructs the concerned officials to immediately coordinate with each other for
the timely submission of the duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names of
the taxpayers and amount due and collectible from each of them to the
Municipal Accounting Office; and

2. Directs the Municipal Accountant to use the duly certified list of taxpayers
showing the names of the taxpayers and amount due and collectible from each of
them as provided by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer at the start of each
year to serve as basis in setting-up the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax
Receivables, in compliance with Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs,
Volume L.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal
Accountant to:

a) Continue to exert extra effort to trace back prior years’ records and documents
which gave rise to the accounts payable;

b) Submit to the Municipal Treasurer those obligations found to be valid and
properly supported by sufficient evidence for immediate payment;

¢) Recorded payables without valid documentations and/or which have been
outstanding for two years or more and against which no actual claim has been
filed should be reverted with the approval of the Sanggunian Bayan to
Government Equity;

d) Record only payables with proper and complete documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to:

a) Reconcile the recorded loan balance with those recorded in the books of the creditor
agency; and

b) Coordinate directly with the DOF-MDFO to determine the cause/s of the
difference. Thereafter, the necessary adjustment/s to correct the errors committed
either in the books of the Municipal Government or in the records of the DOF-
MDFO should be made to reflect the correct amount of the outstanding obligation
of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office continues to remit
the monthly GSIS contributions and loan amortizations withheld from employees in
full and within the required period to avoid penalties, such as interests and surcharges
that maybe imposed by the GSIS, and the possible non-approval of loans or payments
of benefits of retiring personnel.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Directs the concerned officials to replenish the charges made from the SEF by
transferring funds from ihe Generai fund in the ioiai amount of £1,036,920.00
thru a resolution duly approved by the Sangguniang Bayan; and

b) Refrain from approving disbursements if such are not among the eligible
expenses chargeable against the SEF in order to ensure proper utilization of

the said fund and to avoid audit disallowance in the future.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Requires the Bids and Awards Committee to send out written invitations to
COA and at least two (2) other observers at least three calendar days before the
date of the procurement stage/activity to be witnessed; and

b) Instruets the BAC Secretariat to be compliant in posting procurement/bidding
notices in the PhilGEPS and in the Municipality’s website as required by
R.A. 9184 to attain the widest circulation of the agency’s procurement/bidding
activities

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Directs the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to discharge her
functions efficiently, study the projects under 20% Development Fund, closely
monitor the implementation of those ongoing ones;

b) Ensures that implementation of development projects is kept on track for
optimum utilization of the funds allocated therefor; and

¢) Continues to invest in high-impact or developmental projects for inclusion in
the Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instructs the BUB-Focal
Person and the concerned municipal officials to expedite the implementation of the
BUB programs and projects in accordance with the terms and conditions of the MOA
for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries.
